title: erigeron acris subsp. pycnotrichus
intro: Erigeron acris subsp. pycnotrichus, also known as the mountain fleabane, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to high mountain regions. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Erigeron acris subsp. pycnotrichus include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as potentially reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used in traditional medicine as a poultice or topical application for skin conditions.
